name: Internet & Tech
description: The web, memes, apps, and modern tech trivia

questions.q: What does 'WWW' stand for?

source.notes: Invented by Tim Berners-Lee in 1989 at CERN.

questions.q: What internet slang does 'LOL' stand for?

source.notes: Added to Oxford English Dictionary in 2011. Sometimes 'Laugh Out Loud.'

questions.q: What does 'NVM' typically stand for in texting?

source.notes: Used to dismiss a topic or cancel a request.

questions.q: How many zeroes are in a 'googol' (the number that inspired Google's name)?

source.notes: Coined by Milton Sirotta, a 9-year-old nephew of mathematician Edward Kasner in 1920.

questions.q: What does the 'AOL' in the former Internet service provider stand for?

source.notes: Founded 1985. Famous 'You've got mail!' audio. Peaked around 2000.

questions.q: Who is credited with inventing the World Wide Web in 1989?

source.notes: British engineer at CERN. Created HTTP, HTML, and URLs.

questions.q: What does 'RSS' stand for in web feed terminology?

source.notes: Web feed format. Allows users to subscribe to websites for automatic content updates.

questions.q: What was Google's original unofficial corporate slogan?

source.notes: Used from 2000 onward. Replaced with 'Do the right thing' in 2015 under Alphabet.

questions.q: Which search engine was a major competitor before Google's rise?

source.notes: Launched 1995 by DEC. One of the first major full-text search engines on the web.

questions.q: What messaging service was one of the first to popularize instant messaging in the late 1990s?

source.notes: Launched 1996 by Israeli company Mirabilis. Name is pun on 'I Seek You.'

questions.q: What was the first social network to reach 1 billion monthly active users?

source.notes: Reached 1 billion in October 2012. Facebook was founded in 2004.

questions.q: What year was the first iPhone released?

source.notes: Steve Jobs announced it January 9, 2007. Sales began June 29, 2007.

questions.q: What popular early-2010s meme featured a photo of a cat with a permanently displeased expression?

source.notes: Real name Tardar Sauce. Died in 2019. Owner earned millions from appearances.

questions.q: What does 'HTTP' stand for?

source.notes: Foundation of data exchange on the web. HTTPS adds encryption via SSL/TLS.

questions.q: What programming language, despite the name, has nothing to do with coffee-related Java?

source.notes: Created in 10 days by Brendan Eich at Netscape in 1995. Originally called LiveScript.

questions.q: Who founded Facebook (now Meta) in 2004?

source.notes: Co-founded with Eduardo Saverin, Dustin Moskovitz, Chris Hughes, Andrew McCollum at Harvard.

questions.q: What was Twitter's original character limit for tweets before it was doubled in 2017?

source.notes: 140 chars reflected SMS limits. Increased to 280 in November 2017. X now has 280 free, more for paid.

questions.q: Who is the CEO of SpaceX and acquired Twitter (now X) in 2022?

source.notes: Musk acquired Twitter for $44 billion in October 2022 and renamed it X.

questions.q: Which search engine created by Microsoft launched in 2009 as a rebrand of Live Search?

source.notes: Launched June 2009. Rebrand was preceded by Live Search (2006-2009).

questions.q: What does 'SSL' stand for (the technology that encrypts web traffic)?

source.notes: Largely replaced by TLS (Transport Layer Security) but the term SSL persists.
